headline: Ben-Gvir's video of European activists exhibits his policy of ill-treating Palestinian prisoners
description: Israel's far-right National Security Minister sparked international outcry and was even rebuked by the US ambassador and Benjamin Netanyahu after he posted footage of European activists held with heads to the ground and hands bound.

headline: 'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique

Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
description: Candidates backed by Donald Trump for the Republican Party nomination in the November midterm elections benefit from the mobilization of the MAGA base, which remains loyal to the billionaire despite his declining popularity.

headline: Xi, at center of global affairs, keeps Putin waiting on Power of Siberia 2 pipeline
description: Vladimir Putin, who, after Donald Trump, came to Beijing to celebrate the 25th anniversary of the China-Russia friendship treaty, failed to secure the firm commitment he had hoped for from Xi Jinping regarding the pipeline, which is intended to carry gas from northwestern Russia to China.

headline: Who is Emmanuel Moulin, the new governor of France's central bank?
description: Parliament approved the appointment of the former Elysée chief of staff to head the Banque de France on Wednesday, May 20. Opponents criticized his closeness to both Nicolas Sarkozy and Emmanuel Macron.

headline: JR covers Pont-Neuf bridge in Paris with giant inflatable cave
description: The temporary installation 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf' has been set up on the Paris landmark since Thursday. The site will be open to the public free of charge, 24 hours a day, for three weeks from June 6.

description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

headline: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
description: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

headline: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
description: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.
